experiences.intentions: The board is nearly empty except for a Z resting at the bottom right and the active S high near the top center-left. Early game goal is to place pieces cleanly to build a flat stack and avoid creating holes. Immediate options are to shift, rotate, or drop. Since there is no nearby obstruction, the best sub-goal is to move the S toward the left side to keep the center/right open for upcoming J, L, O, I pieces and potentially pair shapes without stacking awkwardly over the bottom-right Z. A single left step starts that repositioning while preserving flexibility; dropping now would commit too early, and rotating is unnecessary on an empty field.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J is near the top in spawn orientation, with a low uneven stack at the bottom: S on rows 16-17 left-center and Z on rows 18-19 right-center, creating a shallow central valley. Goal is to place the J to improve the surface and preserve flexibility for upcoming L,O,I,T. Immediate hard drop in current orientation would likely land awkwardly across the center. Rotating to a vertical J can fit more cleanly into the developing contours; among one-step actions, rotating right advances toward that setup without committing horizontally yet.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J piece is high near the top in vertical orientation at columns around 5, while the stack is only at the bottom with an S/Z pattern leaving a shallow central-right surface. There is no immediate threat of topping out, so the goal is to place the J to build a flatter foundation for upcoming L,O,I,T pieces. A horizontal J would fit well across the upper side of the current bottom stack if moved later, but with only one action now the most progress comes from dropping the piece closer to the stack while preserving flexibility; horizontal movement or rotation can still be done on later ticks. hard_drop would commit too early from the spawn column, and left/right/rotate are less urgent than descent given the empty space below. Therefore soft_drop is best.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty, with only low S/Z stacks near the center-bottom. The active J is high and currently vertical; there is no immediate threat, so the goal is to position it efficiently for a clean, flat stack and future I/T placement. Moving left now starts guiding the J toward the left side of the central mound, where it can later be rotated/dropped to smooth the surface better than staying centered or moving right. Rotating immediately is less urgent because horizontal movement is easy while the piece is high, and dropping now would commit too early.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J is high above a low, centered stack made of S and Z pieces, with the middle columns currently the safest landing zone. From the current vertical orientation, the strongest immediate move is to bring it down quickly; lateral shifts or rotations can still be adjusted later, but the first priority is advancing the piece toward the stack. A hard drop would commit too early from a high position, while soft_drop preserves flexibility and moves toward placement.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is near the top in its spawn orientation. The stack is very low, with surface heights roughly 3 on the left, 4-5 in the center, and 2 on the right, so there is no immediate threat. Goal is to place pieces cleanly and preserve a flat surface while setting up the upcoming O and I. Among immediate actions, moving right shifts the L toward the low right side where it can later be dropped to fill the notch above the Z area; moving left would worsen the higher left side, rotating now is premature without horizontal alignment, and dropping immediately would lock it high and waste placement flexibility. So the best single action now is to move right.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the upper-left, currently vertical (rotation 1), with plenty of empty space below. The stack is low and uneven mainly on the left/center, and there is no immediate danger. Goal is to place the Z efficiently to keep the surface flat and avoid creating holes. Since the piece is still high, the best immediate sub-goal is to shift it toward the left wall where a Z can likely fit more cleanly over the existing left-side contours than dropping now in place. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would commit too early in a mostly empty upper field; rotations just toggle between horizontal and vertical without clear immediate benefit; right moves away from the useful left structure. Moving left improves future placement options while preserving flexibility.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active I piece is horizontal near the top at row 4, while the stack below has a left-side cavity and a relatively flat, open right side. Immediate threats are low because the piece is high with plenty of space. My goal is to place this I to improve future line clears without creating overhangs. Candidate actions: rotate_right makes it vertical, but there is no urgent deep well directly under its current column and vertical I is usually more committal. Left/right would reposition, but with only one action now the strongest progress toward a useful placement is to start dropping into the open field. Soft_drop advances safely while preserving later steering options; hard_drop would commit too early. So the best action now is soft_drop.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a horizontal I near the top with an otherwise low, uneven stack below. There is already a horizontal I at row 13 creating a wide shelf, and columns 4-5 are relatively open above the central structure. Goal is to place the current I efficiently without creating overhangs, ideally using the vertical gap potential in the center-right. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place the horizontal I on top of the highest available surface and likely increase flat clutter; moving left/right first could set up later, but with one action now the strongest immediate improvement is rotating vertical to access a narrow column and preserve flexibility. Soft drop is less committal but does not improve shape as much. Therefore rotate the I clockwise into vertical orientation.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I in vertical orientation at column 5, high above the stack. The surface below has a deep open left well and a low right side, but no immediate need to commit vertically here. My goal is to place the I to improve board smoothness and avoid creating inaccessible holes; with next pieces J,O,T,S, preserving flexibility matters. Candidate actions: hard_drop would lock the I vertically around column 5, which doesn't clear lines and may leave awkward height; soft_drop just delays lateral positioning; rotations to horizontal now are possible, but first shifting left is strongest because it starts moving toward the broad open area where the I can later be laid flat or positioned more usefully. Right moves toward a more obstructed side. So the best immediate action is left.

Candidate actions: soft/hard drop would commit the vertical I into a less useful well; left/right without rotation delays the stronger setup. Best action now is rotate_right.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the left-center in horizontal orientation. Surface below has a pronounced vertical well around columns 2-3 above the left stack, and there is no immediate danger since the stack is only mid-height. Goal is to place the J to improve the left-side contour without creating overhangs; moving left lets the current horizontal J line up better with the left structure and preserve future placements, while hard dropping now in the current column would leave it floating awkwardly on the central terrain. Rotation is not yet necessary before shifting toward the target area.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the top-left in rotation 0. The stack below has a left-side channel and a fairly flat center-right already occupied by vertical I and O blocks; there is no immediate threat at the top, so this turn should improve future placement rather than panic-drop. A good sub-goal is to move the J toward the left wall where J pieces often fit the stepped left structure around the lower rows. From the current spawn position, shifting left preserves flexibility for later rotations and drops better than hard/soft dropping immediately. Rotating now is premature without horizontal alignment, and moving right heads away from the useful left contour.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high on the far left with lots of empty space below; there is no immediate threat at the top. The stack is concentrated in the lower half, with the left wall having a jagged column and an open vertical channel near columns 2-3. Goal is to place this J to improve surface and avoid wasting the early-game open board. Rotating now to vertical and then dropping along the left side is promising; among single moves, rotate_right changes the piece into a vertical J that can later slot more cleanly down the left edge than the current flat orientation. Left is impossible at the wall, hard/soft drop in current orientation would strand it high and wide, and right is less committal than needed.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J in rotation 1 near the upper-left, with open space below and a somewhat uneven left stack. Immediate threats are low since the stack height is modest and there is plenty of air. Goal is to place the J to improve the left side without creating overhangs; since only one action is allowed now, the best sub-goal is to advance the piece downward while preserving flexibility for later alignment with the left structure. Candidate moves left/right adjust horizontally but there is no urgent need yet; rotations are possible but current vertical J already fits the left contour. A hard drop would commit too early, while soft_drop safely progresses toward placement and keeps future options open.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high on the left with a vertical well around columns 0-3; the stack is uneven but not in immediate danger. Goal is to place this J to improve the left surface without creating holes and keep options for upcoming O,T,S,L. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would lock the current awkward vertical orientation too early; rotations could create a flatter J shape, but first shifting left is strong because the left wall offers good placements for multiple J orientations and preserves flexibility for a later rotate/drop. Moving right drifts toward the central I column and risks bumpier stacking. So pursue positioning toward the left wall first.

Surface has a clear 4-cell-wide trench on the far right at row 13 that can be used later, while current piece is already resting just above uneven stacks. Immediate goal is to place the I cleanly without creating extra overhangs. Moving right shifts the horizontal I toward the open right side and away from the left clutter, setting up a safer eventual drop; left would worsen congestion, rotations are invalid except staying as-is, and dropping now would lock suboptimally.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is O at the very top, currently centered in columns 4-5 with no rotation effect. The stack is uneven but low enough that immediate danger is minimal. Since O cannot benefit from rotation and moving it horizontally before committing is usually best, a good sub-goal is to position it toward one side to fill surface gaps. From the current board, the right side has a clean 2-wide vertical channel above the existing O blocks around rows 14-15, so shifting right starts aligning the O for a useful placement. Candidate actions: rotate does nothing for O; hard/soft drop would commit too early in the current column; left is possible but less immediately synergistic than stacking over the right-side O area. So the best move now is right.

The stack is uneven but there is open vertical space directly below the O piece, and no immediate line clear setup from lateral movement in one step. Goal is to place the O efficiently and advance toward a flatter surface; since O cannot benefit from rotation and horizontal repositioning would take multiple turns, the best immediate progress is to drop it downward safely. Hard drop secures the piece quickly into the available central gap.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active T is high near the center top, while the stack below is low-to-mid with a fairly flat central area and some unevenness on the left. Immediate threats are low since the board top is open. Goal now is to improve surface fit and move toward future clean placements with upcoming S,L,L,I pieces. A good sub-goal is to shift the T toward the center-left where it can later be dropped into the open notch/flat area rather than committing immediately from spawn. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would place too early from a poor spawn column; rotations do not create an urgent benefit yet; right moves away from the more useful central-left terrain. Moving left starts aligning the T with the more favorable placement zone while preserving future options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high near the center in spawn area with no immediate collision threat. The stack below is uneven, with a relatively open center-left and no urgent need to hard commit. Goal is to position the T for a useful placement while preserving flexibility for upcoming S,L,L,I. Comparing moves: hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early from a high spawn; rotations don't create a clear immediate advantage yet; left would move toward the taller left-side clutter. Moving right shifts the T toward the more open central-right area and keeps future options for rotation and placement over the existing wells.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near center in spawn area. Surface below has a left-side cavity around columns 3-5 above the stack; immediate goal is to move toward a clean placement rather than drop centrally onto uneven terrain. Rotating now doesn’t create a better transit shape than the current flat-bottom T, and hard/soft drop from here would commit too early. Shifting left starts aligning the T with the central-left gap and preserves future rotation options.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high and centered in a mostly empty upper field. The stack below has uneven columns with a notable vertical gap around column 3–4 and relatively low immediate danger, so this is a setup move rather than emergency. Goal is to place T efficiently without creating extra holes; dropping now from spawn in its current orientation will likely land across the central surface and can tuck into the mild central depression better than spending a turn shifting first. Candidate moves: left/right start repositioning but with no urgent need and less information about final fit in one step; rotations change profile but current flat-top T is already versatile. Since the field is safe, best progress is to commit downward and preserve tempo with a hard drop.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top at columns 4-5. Surface below is uneven, with a T bump around column 4 and substantial open air above; there is no immediate threat at the top. Goal is to place the S efficiently to reduce future holes and prepare for upcoming L,L,I,S. Rotating right changes the S to vertical, which can better fit around the existing bump and gives more placement flexibility before dropping. Left/right now are less informative without committing, and hard/soft drop in the current horizontal orientation would likely leave a flatter but less adaptable placement high above open space. Best sub-goal is improve orientation first.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top in rotation 1, with plenty of vertical space below. The stack is uneven but not near the top, so immediate threat is low. Goal is to place the S efficiently to reduce surface roughness and avoid creating holes, while keeping options open for upcoming L,L,I,S. A good sub-goal is to orient the S horizontally so it can later slide into a notch or land flatter; rotating now is safe because there is no obstruction around the piece. Candidate moves: left/right only shift without improving fit yet, soft/hard drop would commit prematurely in the current less-flexible vertical orientation, and no_op wastes tempo. rotate_left changes from current rotation 1 to horizontal rotation 0, improving placement flexibility immediately.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top at columns 4-6. The stack is low-mid with no immediate top-out threat, and there’s no need to commit horizontally yet without seeing the exact landing. Immediate goal is to improve placement options and descend toward a useful slot. Rotating to vertical S creates a narrower profile that can fit future notches, but first advancing downward preserves flexibility and progresses the piece toward the stack. Left/right now is premature; hard drop is risky without alignment. Soft drop is the safest constructive move.

experiences.intentions: Current piece is S near the top center in horizontal orientation, with open space below and a T-stack/irregular surface around columns 4-6. Immediate threats are low since the stack is far from the top. Goal is to place the S efficiently without creating holes and ideally match the contour. Candidate actions: rotate_right makes the S vertical, which could fit the notch around columns 5-6 better; left/right would only shift before deciding orientation; dropping now in horizontal form would likely rest less cleanly. Since there is time and rotation appears valid, best sub-goal is to orient the piece for a cleaner future placement. Therefore rotate_right.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is high at the top with plenty of horizontal freedom and no immediate danger. The stack below is uneven, with a notable left-side cavity/step structure and a relatively open center-right. My goal now is to improve future placement flexibility and avoid committing the L in its current top-heavy horizontal shape too early. Candidate actions: hard_drop would lock far too early without careful alignment; soft_drop reduces flexibility before orienting; left/right could be useful, but first rotating to a vertical L gives better access to columns and future tuck options. Between rotations, rotate_right to orientation 1 makes the piece a compact vertical shape that is generally easier to steer into the left step or center wells on subsequent moves.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high near the top in rotation 1, around columns 5-6. Surface below is uneven but there is open air, so immediate threat is low. Goal is to place the L efficiently without creating a bad overhang while setting up for upcoming I/S/Z. Comparing actions: lateral moves are premature without seeing final drop path; hard_drop commits too early from a high position; soft_drop usefully advances the piece downward while preserving future steering/rotation options. Rotations could help later, but current orientation is already compact enough for descent. Best sub-goal now is to bring the piece closer to the stack safely.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active L is high and near center-right in vertical orientation. Surface below has a shallow valley around columns 4-6 with some support, and there is no immediate line clear available from an instant drop. Goal is to place the L to improve surface for upcoming L,I,S,Z while avoiding creating holes. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would commit a likely suboptimal high placement from the spawn column; soft_drop also commits downward before aligning. Rotations are possible but current orientation is already workable. Moving left aims the piece toward the central cavity/step where L can tuck more cleanly than on the right, preserving flexibility for the upcoming I piece. Right would drift toward a less useful overhang. Best sub-goal now is lateral alignment before dropping, so choose left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top center. The stack is low and uneven, with a useful cavity around columns 4-6 above the existing S/T structure. Immediate goal is to place the L cleanly without creating overhangs, and a vertical orientation is promising. Among single-step actions, rotating right changes the current horizontal L into a vertical form that can be dropped into the center lane on a following turn; moving first is less committal but misses the better fit setup. Hard/soft drop now in the spawn orientation would likely create a flatter but less efficient placement. So rotate to orientation 1 first.
